title: People tab — contact CRM with interaction log and follow-up queue
description: Added a People tab — a lightweight personal CRM built into amux. Contacts are stored in SQLite with fields for company, role, email, LinkedIn, phone, and rich notes. Each contact has a health indicator (green/yellow/red/never) based on days since last interaction, a full interaction log (calls, emails, meetings, DMs, events), and a follow-up queue surfaced at the top of the sidebar. New contacts get auto-assigned PPL-N IDs using the same sequence infrastructure as the board. The UI is fully optimistic with AbortController-guarded fetches and in-place list patching — no flicker, no full re-renders. Also accessible via CLI: `amux crm list`, `amux crm add`, `amux crm log`, `amux crm fu`.

title: Notes sidebar panel icons, offline save queuing, title sync
description: Replaced the text chevrons (‹ ›) in the notes sidebar toggle buttons with proper shadcn-style Lucide PanelLeftClose / PanelLeftOpen SVG icons, matching the rest of the amux UI. When offline, note saves are now queued and show a 'Queued' status indicator instead of the misleading 'Saved'. The list refresh after a save is skipped when offline to avoid fetch errors. Title changes made in the title input now sync immediately to the sidebar list without waiting for a server round-trip.

title: Notes sidebar collapse/expand + mobile full-width panel switching
description: The notes sidebar can now be collapsed to free up editor space. A toggle button in the sidebar header collapses it to zero width with a smooth CSS transition. An expand button appears in the editor header when collapsed. On mobile (≤600px), the sidebar becomes a full-width absolute overlay that slides in and out via CSS transform, while the editor remains full-width underneath. Sidebar open/closed state is persisted in localStorage so it survives page reloads.

title: Fix notes title overwrite — bidirectional H1 sync, flush save on switch
description: Fixed a bug where changing the note title and then switching to another note would lose the change. The fix has two parts: (1) bidirectional sync between the title input and Quill's H1 heading — typing in the title input updates the H1 in Quill, and editing the H1 directly in Quill updates the title input; (2) pending debounced saves are now flushed synchronously before switching notes, ensuring no content is lost when rapidly switching between notes.

title: Fix notes sidebar click broken by JSON.stringify double-quote conflict
description: Fixed a bug where clicking notes in the sidebar did nothing. The root cause was that note paths were embedded in onclick attributes using JSON.stringify, which wraps strings in double quotes — breaking the surrounding HTML double-quoted attribute. The fix replaces the inline onclick pattern with data-path attributes and this.dataset.path lookups, which is both correct and XSS-safe.

title: Quill WYSIWYG editor for notes + pin to top
description: Replaced the plain textarea + markdown preview with Quill v2 WYSIWYG editor. The toolbar supports headings, bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, blockquote, code block, ordered/unordered lists, and links — no markdown syntax required. Notes can now be pinned to the top of the sidebar list with a pin icon that appears on hover. Pinned notes are visually indicated with a left accent border and a persistent pin icon. Pin state is stored in a hidden .pins.json file in the notes directory.

title: Notes tab — create, edit, search, and manage Markdown notes
description: Introduced the Notes tab — a full note-taking interface inside amux. Notes are stored as Markdown files in ~/.amux/notes/ and support full CRUD operations. The sidebar lists all notes sorted by last-modified time with a search/filter input. Notes are named by their H1 heading; if no heading is present, the filename stem is used. All note mutations go through the offline queue (apiCall) so edits made without connectivity are replayed when the server comes back.

title: Live search/filter in session explorer and Files tab
description: Added real-time search filtering to the session explorer and Files tab. In sessions, typing in the search bar immediately filters visible cards by session name and directory path. In Files, a search input filters by filename and path. Both filters work client-side with no server round-trips, making filtering instant even with hundreds of sessions or files.

title: Pin files and directories to top of session file explorer
description: Files and directories in the session file explorer can now be pinned to the top of the listing. A pin icon appears on hover; clicking it persists the pin in a .pins.json file in the session's home directory. Pinned items are always sorted first, separated visually from the rest of the list, making it easy to keep important files like READMEs and config files accessible.

title: Scheduler tab — recurring tasks, run history, free-text schedule expressions
description: Added a Scheduler tab for defining recurring tasks that run on a cron-like schedule. Schedules are defined using free-text expressions (e.g. 'every 5 minutes', 'daily at 9am') which are parsed into cron format. Each scheduled task can target any active session. The tab shows a full run history with timestamps and status. Tasks can be paused, resumed, or deleted individually.

title: Auto-restart Claude when it exits to a shell prompt (context escape hatch)
description: When Claude reaches its context limit, it exits back to the shell with a message like 'claude>'. amux now detects this shell prompt pattern and automatically re-launches Claude in the same session without any manual intervention. This means long-running Claude sessions no longer stall — they seamlessly continue from where the context was exhausted, keeping the workflow uninterrupted.

title: Auto-configure Claude for cloud containers — skip setup wizard
description: Cloud containers now automatically configure Claude Code at startup using the user's Anthropic API key, skipping the interactive setup wizard that would otherwise block the session. The _init_claude_config() function writes ~/.claude.json directly with the correct OAuth/API key, so Claude is ready to use immediately when a cloud session is first opened.

title: Gateway-level org/invite system with workspace switching
description: Added a full organization and invitation system to the amux cloud gateway. Users can create workspaces, invite members by email, and switch between workspaces. The gateway handles org membership validation, invite tokens, and workspace routing — proxying each request to the correct user container based on the active workspace. Members management is exposed in the settings panel.

title: Collapse template section by default in create session modal
description: The template selector in the Create Session modal is now collapsed by default, reducing visual clutter for users who don't use templates. A chevron toggle expands it when needed. This makes the modal feel lighter and faster to interact with for the common case.

title: Offline-first PWA — HTTPS, service worker, draft sessions, sync engine
description: Transformed amux into a full offline-first Progressive Web App. Features include: a service worker that caches the app shell and API responses; HTTPS via self-signed cert with automatic Tailscale cert fallback; draft session creation that queues when offline and syncs on reconnect; an IndexedDB-backed sync engine that stores board issues and sessions locally; SSE push updates that write to IDB and trigger UI refreshes; and an offline banner with a visible queue of pending operations.
